title: Human Gates, Permissions, and Reversible Autonomy · Recovery Bay
summary: Set human review points for a consequential operations agent. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a risk-tiered approval matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify actions by reversibility, blast radius, sensitivity, and external impact.
Place approvals immediately before consequential effects with enough evidence to decide.
Give each loop and tool only the capabilities and data required for its current goal.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Publish the incident record

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.
